Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

don't like patch at all. MGC need rewrited to use pinger to connect import as other "client" does. it's simple but that way open bug with sending limit for lock enqueue. it's have hardcoded value and it's need to be fixed first.

Patch Set 5:

same bug with delay limit is easy reproduce with several addresses for MGS and MGS not on primary address, so we don't able to calculate an limit to waiting and need infinity wait to take lock, but it's too bad as server node able to read config from local storage. To solve it we need rewrite an setup code to read local config first (if possible) and put config log in re-queue list to update when it's possible. in that case client will wait an completion from an take a config from network, but server will read local copy and start immediately. But it's have an issue - mgc don't update an local copy of configs if mgc don't able to update an local config if first connection is unsuccessful or log put to re-enqueue (mgc also lost an wakeup event in that area).

I have patch for a first issue (when mgc don't able to update and local copy config and lost wakeup), and patch to solve it issue with reworking to use pinger. But don't have patches to solve two issues between it.

parents.subject: LU-618 llite: IO accounting of page read

commit.subject: LU-5020 llite: don't reconnect MGC if connecting
commit.message:
LU-5020 llite: don't reconnect MGC if connecting

1. In mgc_set_info_async(KEY=KEY_INIT_RECOV_BACKUP "init_recov_bk"),
   the MGC import should be reconnected only if its state is
   LUSTRE_IMP_DISCON

2. in mgc_target_register, if the target will regenerate the config,
   we should use some longer delay limit to wait the MGC to connect
   to MGS for the target (server) will fail to exit if the request
   expired due to delay limit.

3. In case of parallel mount, the async cleanup of OSS will affects
   the following mount for the OSS can't be setup again, then there
   should be some barrier to sync with the OSS cleanup.

Change-Id: I805b84cf12100ec2cc68f95bb65a9c396e0fbc1b
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/10229
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
